entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Early 86hp K9K variant for NV200 launch; reliable but injector and timing chain tensioner issues emerged on high-mileage units.

commonFailures: Turbo oil seal leaks on high-mileage vans

compatibleVariants: Panel Van M20 1.5 dCi 110hp, Combi 1.5 dCi 110hp, High Roof Panel Van 1.5 dCi 110hp
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging on stop-start urban use

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Naturally aspirated 110hp petrol option for NV200; less common than diesel but timing chain stretch is the dominant failure mode.

entries.title: Electric Motor

commonProblems.h2: Nissan NV200 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Nissan NV200's K9K 1.5 dCi diesel and HR16DE 1.6 petrol engines dominate UK replacement demand, with timing chain tensioner failure, turbo issues, and injector "black death" being the most common failure points. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare repair vs replacement costs for your specific NV200.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why K9K and HR16DE Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Nissan NV200 Panel Van/Combi 1.5 dCi (2009-2019, K9K 628/636/646/892), 1.6 Petrol (2010-2019, HR16DE)
problems.rootCause: The K9K's rear-mounted timing chain tensioner can wear or stick, allowing chain slack that causes rattling, misfire, and potentially catastrophic valve damage if ignored.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Chain/Tensioner Replacement - Replace chain, tensioner, guides before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove timing cover, replace chain kit, reset timing, test run
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before valve contact; adds 40k-60k miles

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Cylinder Head Repair - Repair valve damage after chain jump
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace bent valves, re-machine seat, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Only if block is undamaged; still risks future chain issues

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete replacement with upgraded components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner, new turbo, tested injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution; eliminates repeat failure risk

problems.recommendation: On a Nissan NV200 worth £3,000-£8,000, if you hear chain rattle before catastrophic failure, a preventative tensioner/chain replacement at a specialist offers the best value. If the engine has already jumped time or seized, a rebuilt K9K with upgraded components is the only sensible choice.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K9K engine prices for your Nissan NV200

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Common on High-Mileage K9K Diesel NV200s
problems.affectedModels: Nissan NV200 Panel Van/Combi 1.5 dCi (2011-2019, K9K 636/646/892)
problems.rootCause: The K9K's small turbocharger suffers from oil coking and bearing wear, especially on vans used for short urban journeys with frequent stop-start driving.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Replacement Only - Fit new/recon turbo without engine removal
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, clean oil lines, fit replacement unit, reset ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine is otherwise healthy; doesn't address underlying oil issues

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with New Turbo - Complete engine replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K9K with new turbo, upgraded oil pump, tested injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage vans; eliminates repeat turbo failure risk

problems.recommendation: If your NV200 has over 100k miles and the turbo has failed, replacing just the turbo is a short-term fix. A rebuilt K9K with new turbo and upgraded oil system offers better long-term reliability for a similar total cost when labour is factored in.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K9K turbo-replacement engines for your NV200

problems.h4: Injector "Black Death" - Carbon Buildup on K9K Diesel Engines
problems.affectedModels: Nissan NV200 Panel Van/Combi 1.5 dCi (2009-2019, all K9K variants)
problems.rootCause: Diesel injector seals degrade over time, allowing fuel to leak into the cylinder head, creating hard carbon deposits that can seize injectors and damage the head.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Injector Seal Replacement - Replace seals and clean carbon before seizure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, clean carbon, fit new copper seals, reset coding
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if injectors are not seized; preventative maintenance

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Cylinder Head Refurbishment - Repair head after injector seizure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, extract seized injectors, re-machine seat, refit with new seals
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is sound; still risks future injector issues

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Refurbished Injectors - Complete replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K9K with refurbished injectors, new seals, upgraded oil pump, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for high-mileage vans with multiple injector issues

problems.recommendation: If you catch injector leaks early (black carbon around injector bases), seal replacement is cost-effective. If injectors are seized or the head is damaged, a rebuilt engine with refurbished injectors offers better value than head repair alone.
problems.cta: Compare K9K engines with refurbished injectors for your NV200

problems.h4: EGR Valve Clogging - Common on Urban-Use K9K Diesel NV200s
problems.affectedModels: Nissan NV200 Panel Van/Combi 1.5 dCi (2010-2019, all K9K variants)
problems.rootCause: The EGR valve recirculates exhaust gases to reduce emissions, but carbon buildup from stop-start urban driving can clog the valve and cooler, causing loss of power and black smoke.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - EGR Cleaning/Replacement - Clean or replace EGR valve and cooler
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove EGR assembly, clean carbon or fit replacement, reset ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for early clogging; doesn't address underlying oil/combustion issues

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Cleaned EGR System - Complete replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt K9K with cleaned EGR system, new seals, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage vans with multiple emissions-related faults

problems.recommendation: For vans under 80k miles with EGR clogging, cleaning or replacing the valve is cost-effective. For higher-mileage NV200s with multiple emissions faults, a rebuilt engine with a cleaned EGR system offers better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for NV200 engines with cleaned EGR systems

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan NV200
finalCta.paragraph: The Nissan NV200 was named the cheapest van to repair in the UK by Tempcover, with average repair bills of just £303.94 - but major engine failures require a different approach. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK NV200 specialists for rebuilt K9K and HR16DE engines, all with upgraded components addressing the original failure point, 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, and nationwide supply & fit available. Compare quotes today and get your van back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan NV200 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan NV200 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

types.title: Nissan NV200 Reconditioned Engines (Recon)

types.frontDisclaimer: Ideal for budget-conscious van owners needing reliable power.

backBullets: Best value for vans under 100k miles

types.frontDescription: Comprehensive rebuild with upgraded components addressing known K9K failure points.

types.title: Nissan NV200 Used Engines

types.backDescription: Used NV200 engines are compression-tested and come with 3-6 month warranty. Ideal for budget repairs on older vans where upfront cost is the priority.

types.frontDescription: Targeted repair of specific faults like turbo or injectors without full rebuild.
types.frontDisclaimer: Suitable when block and head are confirmed sound.
types.backDescription: Refurbished NV200 engines address specific failures like turbo replacement or injector refurbishment while retaining the original block. Cost-effective for known single-point failures.

engineSizes.h2: Nissan NV200 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Nissan NV200 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure points to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Nissan NV200 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L K9K diesel powers the vast majority of UK NV200 vans from 2009-2019, available in 86hp, 90hp and 110hp outputs. This efficient, torquey unit is the backbone of the NV200 range but timing chain tensioner and turbo failures drive most replacement enquiries.
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan NV200 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Nissan NV200 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L HR16DE petrol engine was offered alongside the diesel from 2010-2019 as a lower-cost alternative for lighter-duty NV200 use. Less common than the K9K diesel but timing chain stretch is the dominant failure mode on high-mileage examples.
items.cta: Compare Nissan NV200 1.6L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Nissan NV200 Hybrid / Electrified Engine Sizes

items.title: Nissan NV200 Electric Engines
items.description: The e-NV200 variant features the EM57 electric motor producing 109hp with zero emissions. A separate supply chain from combustion engines with different failure patterns focused on the high-voltage inverter and reduction gearbox.
items.cta: Enquire about Nissan NV200 electric motor availability

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Nissan NV200 needs? items.title: Nissan NV200 Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L K9K diesel powers most UK NV200 vans from 2009-2019, with 86hp, 90hp and 110hp variants dominating replacement enquiries due to timing chain and turbo failures.
items.descriptor: The 1.5L K9K diesel powers most UK NV200 vans from 2009-2019, with 86hp, 90hp and 110hp variants dominating replacement enquiries due to timing chain and turbo failures.
items.familiesLabel: Common Nissan NV200 Diesel Engine Families
families: K9K 628/608 - 86hp early variants (2009-2011), injector and timing chain tensioner risk
K9K 636/646 - 110hp mainstay (2011-2019), turbo failure and EGR clogging common
K9K 892 - 90hp Combi variant (2011-2015), shares failure patterns with other K9K codes
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for commercial van use
Timing chain tensioner failure between 60k-100k miles
Turbocharger wear on high-mileage urban-use vans
High UK replacement demand on Panel Van and Combi variants

items.descriptor: The 1.6L HR16DE petrol was offered 2010-2019 as a lower-cost alternative for lighter-duty NV200 use, with timing chain stretch the dominant failure mode.
items.familiesLabel: Common Nissan NV200 Petrol Engine Families
families: HR16DE - 110hp naturally aspirated petrol (2010-2019), timing chain stretch common on high mileage
knownFor: Lower upfront cost vs diesel but higher fuel consumption
Timing chain tensioner failure 70k-100k miles
Coil pack misfire and oil consumption on older examples
Lower search volume but loyal owner base for petrol variants

families: 80kW permanent magnet synchronous motor drives front wheels

EM57 motor replacement requires battery health inspection
Availability more limited than K9K diesel; longer lead times possible

engineYears.h2: Nissan NV200 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the NV200's production timeline from 2009-2019, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The NV200 launched in the UK with the 86hp K9K 628 diesel as the sole engine option for the Panel Van.
keyChanges: NV200 Panel Van M20 launched in UK market
K9K 628 1.5 dCi 86hp diesel only engine option
Early production units show higher injector failure risk
popularModels: Nissan NV200 Panel Van Low Roof 1.5 dCi
Nissan NV200 Panel Van High Roof 1.5 dCi
knownFor: Named cheapest van to repair in UK by Tempcover (£303.94 avg bill)
Early K9K injector seal issues on 2009-2010 units
Timing chain tensioner wear emerges on high-mileage examples
Strong commercial uptake due to low running costs
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2010 Nissan NV200 replacement engines
years.ticker: NV200 2009 engine replacement • K9K 628 timing chain • NV200 injector failure 2010

years.preview: M20 Matures: 110hp K9K & HR16DE Petrol Arrive
years.description: 2011 brought the higher-output 110hp K9K 636 diesel and the HR16DE 1.6L petrol option, expanding NV200 appeal.
keyChanges: NV200 Combi passenger variant arrives with 7-seat configuration
knownFor: K9K 636 turbo failure risk emerges on high-mileage vans
HR16DE timing chain stretch reported on petrol variants
Combi variant popular with small businesses needing passenger capacity
Euro 5 emissions compliance for 2011-2014 models
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2014 Nissan NV200 replacement engines
years.ticker: NV200 2011 K9K 636 turbo • NV200 HR16DE timing chain • NV200 Combi engine replacement

years.description: 2015 brought Euro 6 K9K 646 diesel for ULEZ compliance, while the e-NV200 electric variant launched in 2014.
keyChanges: K9K 646 Euro 6 diesel introduced for ULEZ compliance
Final NV200 production years for UK market
popularModels: Nissan NV200 Panel Van 1.5 dCi Euro 6
knownFor: Euro 6 K9K requires AdBlue/SCR system maintenance
e-NV200 motor/inverter failures drive electric replacement demand
Late-production NV200s command higher used values
Timing chain issues persist on high-mileage Euro 6 K9K units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2019 Nissan NV200 replacement engines
years.ticker: NV200 Euro 6 engine • e-NV200 EM57 motor • NV200 2015+ K9K replacement

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Nissan NV200 was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Nissan NV200 specialists.

faq.h2: Nissan NV200 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Nissan NV200 engine?
items.answer: Yes, for most business owners. With NV200 values ranging £3,000-£8,000 and replacement engines from £895 (used) to £2,495 (rebuilt), repairing is usually more cost-effective than buying another van.
keyPoints: Used K9K engines from £895 with 3-month warranty
Rebuilt units with new timing chain from £1,895
NV200 named cheapest van to repair in UK (£303 avg bill)
Supply & fit packages minimise business downtime
items.cta: Compare rebuilt NV200 engine prices for your van

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Nissan NV200 engine?
items.answer: UK prices vary by condition: used K9K diesel £895-£1,295, rebuilt K9K £1,895-£2,495, supply & fit labour £300-£500 added to engine price.
keyPoints: Used engines: £895-£1,295 (K9K diesel tested)
Rebuilt engines: £1,895-£2,495 (new timing chain, turbo)
Labour for fitting: £300-£500 at independent specialist
Mobile fitting available for panel vans nationwide
items.cta: Get your Nissan NV200 engine replacement quote today

items.question: What are the most common Nissan NV200 engine problems?
items.answer: K9K diesel timing chain tensioner failure, turbocharger wear, and injector "black death" carbon buildup dominate replacement enquiries for the NV200.
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner fails 60k-100k miles on K9K
Turbo failure common on high-mileage urban-use vans
Injector seal leaks cause carbon buildup around injectors
HR16DE petrol suffers timing chain stretch issues
items.cta: Compare K9K engines with upgraded timing components

items.question: Does the Nissan NV200 K9K engine use a timing belt or chain?
items.answer: The K9K uses a TIMING CHAIN, not a belt. Chain tensioner failure is the known weak point, not the chain itself wearing out.
keyPoints: K9K timing chain is maintenance-free in theory
Tensioner wear allows chain slack causing rattle/misfire
Upgraded tensioners fitted to rebuilt engines prevent repeat failure
Listen for rattling at idle as early warning sign
items.cta: Get quotes for NV200 engines with upgraded timing kits

items.question: How long does a Nissan NV200 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Most K9K diesel replacements take 1-2 days at a specialist garage, with mobile fitting available for panel vans to minimise business downtime.
keyPoints: Standard garage fit: 1-2 working days for K9K diesel
Mobile fitting available at your premises for vans
Next-day engine delivery on most K9K stock units
Full supply & fit packages include road test and handover
items.cta: Find NV200 supply & fit deals with mobile fitting

items.question: What warranty comes with a replacement Nissan NV200 engine?
items.answer: Used engines typically carry 3-6 months warranty; rebuilt/reconditioned units include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard through EngineMarket.
keyPoints: Used K9K: 3-6 months parts warranty standard
Rebuilt engines: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Warranty covers parts and labour at fitting garage
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier
items.cta: Compare NV200 engines with 12-month warranty coverage

items.question: Where can I buy a replacement Nissan NV200 engine in the UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists supplying used, rebuilt and reconditioned NV200 engines with nationwide delivery and supply & fit options.
keyPoints: Compare quotes from 100+ UK engine specialists
All suppliers vetted and rated by real NV200 owners
Nationwide delivery, often next-day on K9K stock
Free comparison tool with no obligation to buy
items.cta: Get free Nissan NV200 engine quotes from UK specialists

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code to my Nissan NV200?
items.answer: Only if ECU, wiring loom and emissions hardware are compatible. Stick to your original K9K variant (628/636/646/892) or HR16DE for guaranteed fitment.
keyPoints: K9K 636 and 646 both 110hp but different ECU mapping
Euro 5 to Euro 6 swap requires emissions system changes
Petrol to diesel conversion not recommended or legal
Enter your reg above to verify exact compatible codes
items.cta: Verify your NV200's compatible engine codes instantly

items.question: Does the Nissan NV200 engine come with supply and fit?
items.answer: Yes, most EngineMarket specialists offer supply & fit packages for NV200 vans, with mobile fitting available to minimise business downtime.
keyPoints: Supply & fit adds £300-£500 to engine price typically
Mobile fitting available at your premises for panel vans
Package includes removal, fitting, fluids and road test
Single-point responsibility simplifies the repair process
items.cta: Find NV200 supply & fit deals with mobile fitting option

items.question: What is the cheapest way to replace a Nissan NV200 engine?
items.answer: A tested used K9K engine from £895 offers the lowest upfront cost, but a rebuilt unit from £1,895 with new timing chain offers better long-term value for high-mileage vans.
keyPoints: Used K9K from £895: lowest cost, 3-month warranty
Rebuilt K9K from £1,895: new timing chain, 12-month warranty
Factor in labour costs when comparing total price
Consider van value and intended use when choosing option
items.cta: Compare cheap NV200 engine options with warranty details
